meaning: adj.难管理的,难处理的,难操纵的,处理不了的
examples: People were visiting the house every day, sometimes in unmanageable numbers.
The signs are that indulged children tend to become unmanageable when they reach their teens.
They feel that the system has become unmanageable.
Sometimes the most unmanageable people are exactly the ones that can have the greatest impact.
Instead, they are seeking government help in covering potentially huge and unmanageable costs from future terrorist acts.
